name: Aurelia
description: A standards-based, convention-over-configuration framework for building high-performing, ambitious applications

description.value: A standards-based, convention-over-configuration framework for building high-performing, ambitious applications

initial-release.comment: Created by Rob Eisenberg after leaving the Angular 2 team at Google in 2014

current-version.comment: Still in beta; Aurelia 2 is the current main version

github-stars.comment: Aurelia 2 repo has ~1.5k stars; Aurelia 1 (aurelia/framework) has ~11.7k stars. As of December 2025.

active-maintenance.comment: Regular releases; Beta 24 in April 2025, Beta 23 in January 2025

reactivity-model.comment: Uses observable/reactive binding without dirty checking; synchronous binding system with observation decorators

component-model.comment: Class-based components with separate HTML templates; uses convention over configuration

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative templates with standard HTML enhanced by Aurelia's binding syntax

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in reactive binding; official State plugin and Store plugin available

opinionatedness.comment: Strong convention-over-configuration philosophy; similar approach to Ruby on Rails

backward-compatibility.comment: Strong API stability commitment; no breaking changes since 2.0

typescript-support.comment: Framework is written in TypeScript; first-class TypeScript support out of the box

template-syntax.comment: Standard HTML templates with Aurelia's binding expressions; no virtual DOM abstractions

styling-approach.comment: No built-in styling solution; works with standard CSS and preprocessors

build-required.comment: Uses npx makes aurelia for scaffolding; Vite/Webpack build required

reactive-primitives.comment: Observation system with @observable decorator and reactive binding

computed-values.comment: Computed properties via getters that auto-track dependencies

two-way-binding.comment: Built-in two-way data binding with .two-way command; also supports .bind which intelligently chooses

event-handling.comment: Declarative event binding with trigger and delegate commands

conditional-rendering.comment: if.bind and show.bind for conditional rendering

component-slots.comment: Content projection via <au-slot> and Shadow DOM slot support

teleport-portal.comment: Portal functionality available for rendering outside component tree

suspense.comment: No built-in Suspense equivalent; async patterns handled differently

context-provide-inject.comment: Powerful dependency injection system is a core feature of Aurelia

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: ref binding for direct DOM element access

nested-routes.comment: Child routing and nested viewports supported

dynamic-routes.comment: Route parameters and dynamic routing supported

route-guards.comment: Router hooks and lifecycle events for navigation guards

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Async loading and code splitting for routes supported

scroll-restoration.comment: Scroll restoration supported via router configuration

built-in-state.comment: Reactive binding provides local component state

built-in-global-state.comment: Official @aurelia/state and @aurelia/store plugins for global state

global-state-library.comment: Official State plugin with middleware support; Store plugin also available

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Aurelia DevTools browser extension supports state inspection

state-persistence.comment: No built-in state persistence; available via community solutions

official-cli.comment: npx makes aurelia for scaffolding new projects

hot-module-replacement.comment: HMR supported via Vite/Webpack dev server

browser-devtools.comment: Official Aurelia DevTools browser extension for Chrome/Firefox

vs-code-extension.comment: Official Aurelia VS Code extension with IntelliSense and completions

error-messages.comment: Helpful error messages with component context; DevTools assist debugging

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs covering fundamentals, routing, plugins, testing; tutorials and guides available

playground.comment: No official online playground; use npx makes aurelia locally

bundle-size-core.comment: Approximately 60 kB gzipped for core runtime; varies with tree-shaking

bundle-size-full.comment: ~100 kB gzipped with router; production builds with tree-shaking are leaner

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; uses direct DOM manipulation based on web standards

compile-time-optimization.comment: Template compiler with optimizations; partial AOT compilation on roadmap

tree-shaking.comment: ES module compatible; bundlers automatically tree-shake unused code

code-splitting.comment: Smart code splitting and async loading supported

lazy-components.comment: Components can be loaded on demand via dynamic imports

ssr-capable.comment: SSR and hydration on 2025 roadmap; improvements in progress

streaming-ssr.comment: Streaming SSR not currently available; SSR improvements in development

partial-hydration.comment: Partial hydration not yet available; on roadmap

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Aurelia not consistently included in recent js-framework-benchmark results; historically performed well (66% behind vanilla JS in Round 4)

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ~5k weekly downloads for aurelia package; relatively small but dedicated user base

component-libraries.comment: aurelia-mdc-web (Material Design), aurelia-ux; smaller ecosystem focuses on quality over quantity

meta-framework.comment: No separate meta-framework; SSR capabilities being built into core

major-companies-using.comment: Used by companies from startups to Fortune 500 since 2015; specific names not publicly disclosed

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven with corporate sponsors; sustainable funding model through individual sponsors, corporate backers, and professional services

discord-community-size.comment: ~122 members in official Aurelia Discord; smaller but engaged community

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Approximately 3,500 questions tagged with aurelia (estimate based on related tag counts)

job-market-demand.comment: Niche demand; used in specific enterprise environments but not widely requested in job postings

official-testing-library.comment: Built-in testing capabilities with comprehensive testing tools

testing-library-name.comment: Official testing utilities for component testing, attributes, and value converters

component-testing.comment: Component testing with stubs, mocks, and spies supported

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress, and other E2E testing tools

snapshot-testing.comment: Snapshot testing supported via Jest or similar tools

mobile-framework.comment: No official NativeScript integration; Capacitor/Cordova work but without dedicated support

pwa-support.comment: PWA tooling documented for desktop apps and PWAs

desktop-framework.comment: Electron and Tauri integration documented for desktop applications

learning-curve.comment: Moderate learning curve; convention-over-configuration helps but requires learning Aurelia-specific patterns

concepts-to-learn.comment: Components, Binding, Dependency Injection, Templating, Routing, Plugins, Value Converters, Lifecycle

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~10 minutes using npx makes aurelia to scaffold and create first component

migration-path.comment: Migration guides available from Aurelia 1 to Aurelia 2; framework specifically designed for smooth upgrades

incremental-adoption.comment: Aurelia is designed as a full framework; not meant for incremental adoption in existing apps
